business.headline: India's affordable housing dream hits a luxury roadblockIndia's housing market is tilting towards luxury projects even as affordable demand remains strong. Experts say rising costs, weak funding and policy gaps are pushing home ownership further from the middle class.

Would you wait 5 years for gratuity or switch for better pay? Indians speakSometimes, the toughest career decisions come with perfect timing or terrible timing, depending on how you see it! Imagine being months away from gratuity when a recruiter calls with an offer too good to ignore. Stay loyal a little longer or leave for better pay? I spoke to Indian professionals, and the answers may surprise you.

Insurers cash in on unlimited health insurance, leave fine print to customersHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the second part of The Unlimited Illusion, we examine what insurers really mean by "unlimited" health cover and why customers often discover the limits only later.

Fuel prices up Rs 5 in 10 days, govt defends limited hike amid West Asia crisisPetrol and diesel prices were raised for the third time this month as the Centre said a 76-day freeze had cushioned consumers despite soaring crude.

Petrol, diesel, CNG prices hiked again. Check rates in your cityIndia relies on imports for nearly 85 per cent of its crude oil requirements, making domestic fuel prices vulnerable to fluctuations in global markets. The fresh hike is likely to put additional pressure on commuters, transporters and businesses across multiple sectors.

India better positioned on inflation, RBI should keep rates unchanged: ASSOCHAMASSOCHAM's analysis showed that India's retail inflation increased only slightly from 3.2% in February 2026 to 3.5% in April 2026 — a rise of just 0.3 percentage points. In comparison, inflation in the United States jumped sharply from 2.4% to 3.8% during the same period.

RBI to transfer record Rs 2.87 lakh crore dividend to govt for FY26This move is expected to give the government additional financial room at a time of geopolitical uncertainty and global economic challenges.

Don't file your ITR blindly: These 6 AIS mismatches can invite an income tax noticeThink filing your ITR is just about checking pre-filled details and clicking 'submit'? Not so fast. As the ITR filing season for FY 2026–27 begins, tax experts say ignoring even a small mismatch in your AIS could land you in trouble later.

Haryana is a home away from home for Japanese firms: CM Nayab Singh SainiSpeaking at the third India-Japan Conclave in New Delhi, organised by India Today, Saini said Haryana has become a "home away from home" for Japanese companies and stressed that the relationship between the two sides is built not only on investment, but also on trust.

8th Pay Commission: Why a full return to old pension scheme may be difficult nowAs the 8th Pay Commission widens consultations, employee unions are renewing their push for the Old Pension Scheme. But with Rs 16.5 lakh crore already inside NPS, the debate is shifting towards guaranteed pension safeguards within the existing system.
Sensex, Nifty end week higher as banking shares drive gainsThe BSE Sensex ended 231.99 points, or 0.31%, higher at 75,415.35. The Nifty 50 gained 64.60 points, or 0.27%, to close at 23,719.30.

Why your next pack of condoms may cost more amid global oil shockThe company, which sells Manforce condoms and holds around 30% market share in the country, said it may eventually pass on higher costs to customers if the situation in the Middle East worsens.

Japan bets big on India with $68 billion investment target, tech pushAt the third India-Japan Conclave organised by the India Today Group, Japan unveiled an updated Indo-Pacific strategy that places India at the heart of its push on technology, investment and regional security.
Sensex up 500 points: Why is stock market rising today?At 1:45 PM, the BSE Sensex was up 514.96 points, or 0.68%, at 75,698.32. The index touched an intraday high of 75,810.97 and a low of 75,230.75.

Is India sleepwalking into a retirement crisis?Many Indians dream of a peaceful retirement, but dreams alone do not pay medical bills or monthly expenses. So, are Indians financially ready for retirement, or just hoping for the best?

From long queues to quick clicks: How innovation is reshaping digital lendingApplying for a loan is no longer just about filling forms and waiting for approvals. From instant verification to quicker disbursals, innovation is reshaping digital lending, but what does this shift really mean for borrowers?

RBI may skip repo rate hike despite rupee fall, eye inflation: ReportThe RBI is expected to focus more on inflation and broader economic stability even as the rupee continues to hit record lows against the US dollar amid the ongoing global oil shock.

Soft landing, not free fall: Decoding RBI's rupee game planThe central bank is increasingly allowing the rupee to weaken gradually as oil prices remain high, with economists arguing that preventing panic matters more than defending a symbolic number.

8th Pay Commission consultation moves to Lucknow: Key dates, eligibility and detailsThe 8th Pay Commission is steadily expanding its consultation process, with Lucknow now added to the list of cities where employee representatives and government bodies can share their views. But who can meet the panel, and what are the key dates you should know?

Rupee at 100 against US dollar? Why experts say India shouldn't panicAs crude oil prices stay elevated and the rupee weakens further, a growing number of experts now argue India's real risk is not crossing 100 against the dollar, but rising inflation and slowing growth.

entertainment.headline: No.1 man always: KJo's emotional note after David Dhawan hints at final film Karan Johar posted an emotional note after David Dhawan said his next film, Hai Jawaani Toh Ishq Hona Hai, may be his last as director. He wrote how David Dhawan is an inspiration for an entire generation of filmmakers.

Karuppu box office Day 9: Suriya's film races towards Rs 250 crore globally Karuppu staged a sharp box-office recovery on its second Saturday, earning Rs 12.20 crore nett in India. The jump pushed the Suriya-starrer's worldwide total to Rs 217.74 crore, with Tamil Nadu leading the collections.

Drishyam 3 box office Day 3: Mohanlal's thriller smashes Rs 100 crore globally Mohanlal's Drishyam 3 has crossed Rs 100 crore worldwide within three days of its release. Strong occupancy rates, led by the Malayalam version, have sustained its weekend run.

Chand Mera Dil box office Day 2: Ananya-Lakshya's film sees strong growth Chand Mera Dil recorded a 21.7 per cent rise in box office collections on its second day. The jump pushed its India nett total to Rs 6.65 crore despite mixed reactions and competition.

Salman Khan is Bollywood's biggest PR nightmare and a wild card Salman Khan's recent social media posts, from backing Rajpal Yadav to rebuking paparazzi, have drawn renewed attention. The burst of candid updates makes one think just how he fits into Bollywood's tightly managed PR culture.

Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce pack on PDA at NBA game amid wedding buzz. Viral video Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce stole the spotlight with their romantic moment at the Cavaliers' Eastern Conference Finals game in Cleveland. The two were seen sharing a kiss during the match.

Actor Rukmini Vasanth files cyber complaint over viral deepfake bikini pics Rukmini Vasanth has filed a cybercrime complaint after AI-generated bikini pictures claiming to be her circulated online. She said the fabricated content violated privacy and highlighted a wider problem affecting many women.

Ram Charan mistakes Bumrah for a footballer at Peddi event, apologises for mix-up Ram Charan apologised after mixing up crivket and football while praising Jasprit Bumrah at the Peddi music launch in Bhopal. In a social media post, the actor wrote how he is forgetful with names sometimes.

Mohanlal's Drishyam films find a unique place in his prolific career. Here's why Mohanlal came into the first Drishyam film of 2013 as an accomplished actor. The challenge was to create a hero that didn't exist before the franchise, and subsequently match the standard in the two sequels.

Aishwarya Rai channels a modern-day swan queen in dramatic white suit at Cannes Aishwarya Rai Bachchan made a striking appearance at Cannes 2026 in an all-white feathered power suit. Her dramatic swan-inspired look, designed by Cheney Chan, stood out for its bold styling and elegant detailing.

Lakshmi aati hai: Desi Bling's viral 'pati seva' moment triggers misogyny debate Desi Bling has sparked debate online after a viral clip featuring Tabinda Sanpal and Satish Sanpal discussing traditional marital practices and beliefs linked to prosperity. The moment has sharply divided viewers, triggering conversations around patriarchy, relationship dynamics, and the kind of lifestyles and values being portrayed on the show.

Drishyam's Ansiba Hassan accuses actor Tiny Tom of calling her jihadi, he reacts Drishyam actor Ansiba Hassan has accused actor Tiny Tom of making defamatory and communal remarks, including allegedly calling her a "jihadi." Tiny Tom has strongly denied the claims, saying the allegations are based on hearsay.

Farhan Akhtar takes Don 3 dispute against Ranveer to film body, decision on Monday Farhan Akhtar has reportedly filed a complaint against Ranveer Singh over his alleged exit from Don 3. The Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) is set to address the matter and announce its decision during a press conference on May 25.

David Dhawan gets emotional recalling Varun during his hospital stay. Watch David Dhawan broke down in tears at the trailer launch of Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai while recalling how son Varun Dhawan stayed beside him during his 2022 hospitalisation.

Salman Khan steals the spotlight at 7 Dogs Egypt premiere as cameo goes viral Salman Khan grabbed attention at the Egypt premiere of 7 Dogs despite skipping the event, as giant posters featuring the actor dominated the venue.

Papa was special: Bobby Deol breaks down remembering Dharmendra Bobby Deol became emotional while remembering father Dharmendra during a conversation with Shekhar Suman on the show Shekhar Tonite. He said people still speak of his father with deep emotion.

Ram Charan's Peddi gets U/A 16+ certificate after minor censor cuts, runtime locked Peddi, starring Ram Charan and Janhvi Kapoor, has received a U/A 16+ certificate ahead of its theatrical release. The makers also revealed the film's final runtime after CBFC-recommended cuts and modifications.

Nepali film Elephants in the Fog makes history, wins Jury Prize at Cannes Elephants in the Fog won the Un Certain Regard Jury Prize at the Cannes Film Festival, becoming the first Nepali film to receive an award at the festival.

Vir Das on Cockroach Janta Party crackdown: Dumb move to shut down their accounts Vir Das called the alleged crackdown on Cockroach Janata Party social media accounts a "dumb move," saying it only strengthened the movement, after founder Abhijeet Dipke claimed several CJP accounts were hacked or withheld.

general news.headline: Marco Rubio teases 'good news in next few hours' as US, Iran near peace dealThe remark came after US President Donald Trump announced that Washington and Tehran were close to finalising a deal aimed at ending the Iran war and reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

Fidayeen attack on Pakistan train kills 24, Baloch Army claims responsibilityThe explosion near Quetta's Chaman Phatak damaged train coaches, nearby homes and vehicles, while the Baloch Liberation Army claimed responsibility, calling it a suicide attack.

Every country has stupid people: Marco Rubio on rising anti-India hate in USMarco Rubio answered a question in New Delhi on racism and attacks against Indians in the US. He said isolated prejudice should not define American society or overshadow thriving Indian-origin communities.

Iran ready to surrender enriched uranium as part of US proposed peace dealIran has agreed in principle to give up its highly enriched uranium stockpile in broader peace talks with the United States. The move could unlock a wider deal on hostilities, enrichment limits and frozen assets.

How a weak rupee is forcing Indian students abroad to skip meals, hitting gradesA falling rupee is forcing Indian students abroad to cut meals, chase about-to-expire discounted food, and take up extra jobs. Their families are facing higher costs to sustain them in the UK or the US, even as the education loans keep ballooning.

Woman carries mother-in-law, 90, on back for 9 km to draw her pension from bankThe video has triggered strong reactions online, with many users questioning why elderly and bedridden pensioners in remote regions are still required to travel long distances for verification despite repeated government claims of doorstep services and Digital India initiatives.

BJP takes huge lead in Bengal's Falta Assembly repoll, Trinamool not in frayDuring the 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections, multiple booths in the constituency came under scrutiny following allegations of electoral irregularities. The Bharatiya Janata Party had accused the TMC workers of large-scale electoral malpractice in the area.

Jaishankar raises US visa issues with Rubio; he says 'not targeted at India'External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar on Sunday said he had taken up the issue of challenges that legitimate travellers face in securing US visas, in his meeting with US counterpart, Marco Rubio, who is currently on an official visit to New Delhi.

AI 101: Use this guide to talk smartly about AI at work, so you don't get firedHandcrafted ByOur Editors Tokenmaxxing. Agent. Inference. We live in an AI world, and it is important you speak the AI language. Or else you will come across clueless at work. Here is a guide that will teach you how to talk smartly about AI.

Watch: Shashi Tharoor's subtle praise for PM Modi on Maldives resetShashi Tharoor cited India's emergency water aid to the Maldives as an example of trust-building diplomacy. He said the response helped soften sentiment despite earlier tensions under Mohamed Muizzu.

They lack basic decency: Udhayanidhi Stalin shreds Congress for TVK tie-upUdhayanidhi Stalin attacked the Congress at a party meeting and blamed it for the BJP's victories. His remarks have intensified focus on strains within the DMK-led opposition alliance.

Over 31,000 Waqf properties' registrations on Umeed portal cancelled in UPOfficials cancelled registrations of 31,328 Waqf properties on the Umeed portal after document scrutiny revealed discrepancies in land records, ownership details, and several claims linked to the properties.

Inside Yogasana, India's bold bid to take yoga to the OlympicsFor years, the perception around yoga was that it was more of a lifestyle and wellness choice. But with a Commonwealth Games push coming up, Yogasana is ready to take one of India's oldest fitness routines to the global stage and possibly, even to the Olympics.

Live: Marco Rubio teases 'good news in next few hours' on US-Iran peace dealUS-Iran-Israel War Live Updates: Iran, US and Pakistan signal major progress in talks to end the nearly three-month-long war, with a Pakistani official involved in negotiations telling Reuters that an interim deal in its final phase is "fairly comprehensive to terminate the war." A memorandum of understanding is being fine-tuned after Pakistan Army chief Asim Munir's talks in Tehran, while sources said the proposed framework includes formally ending the war, reopening the Strait of Hormuz and launching a 30-day negotiation window for a broader agreement. US President Donald Trump said he may decide by Sunday whether to resume attacks on Iran, even as Secretary of State Marco Rubio said "some progress" had been made in ongoing talks.

Why label Indian youth as Pakistani? Cockroach party reacts to minister's postAbhijeet Dipke has countered Kiren Rijiju's suggestion that the Cockroach Janta Party drew support from Pakistan and George Soros-linked networks.

Asked to vacate land, Delhi Gymkhana Club seeks urgent meeting with governmentThe Centre has ordered Delhi Gymkhana Club to hand over its Safdarjung Road premises by June 5. The move has triggered questions from the club, talk of legal action and anxiety among over 600 employees.

Salman Khan is Bollywood's biggest PR nightmare and a wild cardSalman Khan's recent social media posts, from backing Rajpal Yadav to rebuking paparazzi, have drawn renewed attention. The burst of candid updates makes one think just how he fits into Bollywood's tightly managed PR culture.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Arjun makes father Sachin proud in LSG debut. Master pens rare message for son26-year-old Arjun Tendulkar impressed on his debut for Lucknow Super Giants in the team's final game of the IPL 2026. Arjun's impressive spell melted father Sachin Tendulkar's heart who penned a rare message for his son on Saturday.

Day 1 schedule: Novak Djokovic, Mirra Andreeva set to kick off campaignThe French Open 2026 gets underway with Novak Djokovic, Alexander Zverev, Taylor Fritz and Mirra Andreeva headlining the opening-round action across the courts at Roland Garros.
Vikas Bhi, Virasat Bhi: A doctrine that has not yet met its testSomnath shows what India's spiritual economy can build. Char Dham shows what happens when it forgets the mountain underneath.

The cockroach employee: Why Gen Z is built to survive the workplace, not stay loyalHandcrafted ByOur Editors Cockroaches do not survive because they are loyal to one corner of the room. They survive because they adapt fast, move when the space turns harsh, and never stay where they cannot breathe. Many employers now use that same logic to describe Gen Z: a generation that is less interested in staying put and more interested in finding work that feels worth staying for.

India's affordable housing dream hits a luxury roadblockIndia's housing market is tilting towards luxury projects even as affordable demand remains strong. Experts say rising costs, weak funding and policy gaps are pushing home ownership further from the middle class.

Meet the Bangladeshi woman whose food videos are getting rishtas for her sonArifa Begum is based in Bangladesh and cooking is nothing short of a cathartic experience for her. Her food videos have gone viral, so much so that several women have jokingly asked to marry her son Swapnil just to become a part of the family, and, of course, enjoy her delicacies.

sports.headline: Be true to yourself: Guardiola warns Man City against hiring a copy-paste successor Pep Guardiola has told Manchester City not to appoint a manager who tries to replicate him. As he prepares to leave after the Aston Villa match, he said his successor must be unique and backed the club amid the financial charges.

Sanju Samson vs Rohan Bopanna: India stars meet in wild role reversal clash Sanju Samson and Rohan Bopanna entertained fans in a hilarious crossover challenge where cricket met tennis, with both stars stepping into each other's worlds in a fun video.

George Russell pips teammate Kimi Antonelli to take pole for Montreal sprint George Russell took pole for the Canadian Grand Prix sprint as Mercedes locked out the front row with Kimi Antonelli. The result answered a difficult Miami weekend and hinted that Mercedes' upgrade has sharpened its pace.

Pakistan captain downplays hype around Women's T20 World Cup clash vs India Fatima Sana said Pakistan will not focus solely on their high-profile clash against Harmanpreet Kaur's India at the ICC Women's T20 World Cup, stressing that every match in the tournament is equally important.

Inside Yogasana, India's bold bid to take yoga to the Olympics For years, the perception around yoga was that it was more of a lifestyle and wellness choice. But with a Commonwealth Games push coming up, Yogasana is ready to take one of India's oldest fitness routines to the global stage and possibly, even to the Olympics.

Naomi Osaka credits Gael Monfils for inspiring Black tennis talent globally Naomi Osaka praised Gael Monfils for inspiring generations of Black tennis players, saying his success had a huge impact on the sport globally. Ahead of his farewell French Open campaign, Monfils said he was proud to have shown young Black athletes that they too could succeed in tennis.

Rishabh Pant captaincy under threat as LSG consider reset after IPL 2026 Tom Moody suggested that LSG will look to evaluate all their options which includes the leadership positions. This comes after the side led by Rishabh Pant lost to Punjab Kings to finish at the bottom of the points table.

Ruthless Jannik Sinner can be exposed at the French Open. Here's how French Open 2026: Frances Tiafoe believes Jannik Sinner is beatable at the Roland Garros if rivals can unsettle him mentally, despite the Italian being the overwhelming favourite in Carlos Alcaraz's absence.

3-way battle for playoffs. Royals, KKR threaten Punjab in top 4 race Punjab Kings win over LSG on Saturday means that three teams are alive in the IPL playoffs race in the final day of the league stage. Punjab will be keenly watching Rajasthan's match against Mumbai Indians, and Kolkata's match against Delhi on Sunday.

Gurindervir, Vishal, Tejaswin: The night Ranchi witnessed Indian track history At the Birsa Munda Stadium, Gurindervir Singh, Vishal TK, and Tejaswin Shankar shattered three national records, pushing India into elite Asian contention. This is how three athletes altered the trajectory of the nation's track history.

Jannik Sinner's golden run threatens painfully one-sided French Open French Open 2026: World No.1 Jannik Sinner enters the clay-court major in excellent form, having dominated the ATP Tour in recent months. He also has the opportunity to complete a Career Grand Slam, further underlining his rise at the top of men's tennis.

IPL Play of the Day: How 26.75 crore outbid 27 crore in Lucknow's captaincy duelShreyas Iyer led Punjab Kings to a seven-wicket win over Lucknow Super Giants with an unbeaten 101 and sharp captaincy. The result kept Punjab alive in the playoff race and underlined Lucknow's difficult season under Rishabh Pant.

Day 1 schedule: Novak Djokovic, Mirra Andreeva set to kick off campaignThe French Open 2026 gets underway with Novak Djokovic, Alexander Zverev, Taylor Fritz and Mirra Andreeva headlining the opening-round action across the courts at Roland Garros.

Iran shift FIFA World Cup 2026 base from US to Mexico days ahead of kickoffIran have shifted their World Cup 2026 base camp from the United States to Tijuana after Mehdi Taj said FIFA approved the request. The move keeps the team near its US group matches while political and security concerns remain in focus.

Arjun makes father Sachin proud in LSG debut. Master pens rare message for son26-year-old Arjun Tendulkar impressed on his debut for Lucknow Super Giants in the team's final game of the IPL 2026. Arjun's impressive spell melted father Sachin Tendulkar's heart who penned a rare message for his son on Saturday.

Now supporting Mumbai: Shreyas Iyer puts PBKS hopes in MI's handsIPL 2026: Shreyas Iyer's unbeaten century drove Punjab Kings to a seven-wicket win over Lucknow Super Giants. The result leaves PBKS waiting on Mumbai Indians to stop Rajasthan Royals and keep their playoff hopes alive.

Focus on bowling, stop snapchat: Arshdeep Singh gives away 500 runs in IPL 2026Arshdeep Singh's controversial IPL season hit a new low against LSG after the fast bowler conceded 52 runs in just 3 overs. With those 52, Arshdeep gave away more than 500 runs in this edition of the IPL.

Sarpanch Shreyas digs deep to keep Punjab Kings alive in IPL playoffs racePunjab Kings remained alive in the IPL playoff race after snapping their 6-match losing streak. Playing a must-win game against Rishabh Pant's LSG, Shreyas Iyer, Prabhsimran Singh, and Yuzvendra Chahal guided the team to a 7-wicket win.

After IPL final snub, Karnataka announces 80,000-seater stadium near BengaluruKarnataka has unveiled plans for an 80,000-seat cricket stadium near Bengaluru after the IPL 2026 final was moved out of the city. The proposal highlights pressure to expand hosting capacity while Chinnaswamy undergoes redevelopment and faces criticism over crowd management.

RCB dented with huge injury blow ahead of IPL playoffsJacob Bethell has been ruled out of the rest of IPL 2026 after injuring his left ring finger while playing for Royal Challengers Bengaluru. The setback leaves RCB without the batter before Qualifier 1, while England prepare to assess him ahead of the first Test against New Zealand.

2 days, 2 national records: Gurindervir is the fastest Indian aliveIndian sprinting witnessed history in Ranchi as Gurindervir Singh shattered the men's 100m national record twice in two days. The Punjab sprinter became the first Indian ever to breach the 10.10-second barrier during a sensational Federation Cup showdown with Animesh Kujur.

Mohun Bagan refuses to send players for national team, days after ISL heartbreakMohun Bagan have declined to officially release seven players for India's Unity Cup camp before the FIFA window opens on June 1. The move has disrupted Khalid Jamil's preparations and revived the club-versus-country dispute over injury cover.

Playoffs or not, KKR won't change their style vs DC: Head coach Abhishek NayarIPL 2026: Abhishek Nayar said Kolkata Knight Riders will keep the same approach for their must-win match against Delhi Capitals. The result could shape KKR's playoff hopes as Delhi also arrive with an outside chance.

Rishabh Pant apologises to fans, Arjun Tendulkar debuts for LSGIPL 2026, LSG vs PBKS: 26-year-old Arjun Tendulkar has been handed his debut in LSG colours, in the team's final match of the season. Tendulkar was traded to LSG ahead of this season on a straight cash deal.

Jofra Archer not bothered by sharp criticism amid IPL vs England controversyIPL 2026, MI vs RR: Rajasthan Royals assistant coach Trevor Penney said Jofra Archer is unfazed by criticism in the ongoing IPL vs England debate, insisting he remains focused and unaffected by outside noise.

Vinesh Phogat vs WFI: Delhi HC green lights wrestler for Asiad trialsThe Delhi High Court has slammed the Wrestling Federation of India for their vindictive attitude against wrestler Vinesh Phogat. The court has allowed Phogat to compete in the Asian Games trials despite the WFI deeming her ineligible for the competitions.

Why Jasprit Bumrah has struggled in IPL 2026? Mumbai Indians head coach explainsIPL 2026, MI vs RR: Mumbai Indians head coach Mahela Jayawardene said Jasprit Bumrah's struggles were due to a combination of a post–T20 World Cup niggle and the time needed to regain rhythm after injury.

Bruno Fernandes named Premier League player of the season after historic turnaroundManchester United star Bruno Fernandes has been named the Premier League Player of the Season. After a tough start under Ruben Amorim, the midfielder was moved back to his best position by Michael Carrick, matching a historic 20-assist record.

FIFA World Cup: Congo asked to undergo 21-day isolation due to Ebola outbreakCongo's World Cup 2026 preparations have been disrupted after US authorities ordered a 21-day isolation bubble for the squad in Belgium amid an Ebola outbreak in the African nation.

Indian fans may finally have clarity on FIFA World Cup 2026 broadcast rightsIndian football fans may finally get clarity on FIFA World Cup 2026 broadcast rights, with former AIFF general secretary, Shaji Prabhakaran claiming a broadcaster announcement is expected in India next week.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
